former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2) |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sabi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sabi Shrine''' (さびじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ;Main deity *{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} ;Left side hall *{{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|lt=Matsuo Ōkami|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} **{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} **Nakatsushima-hime-no-Kami ;Right side hall *{{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} **{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} **{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Ihahinushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} **{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} **{{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}} Although the honden is in sansha-zukuri form, documents only listed Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto and Matsuo Ōkami, leaving one deity unknown; however, a repair wooden tag from year 6 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} saying "left Matsuo Daimyōjin, right Kasuga Daimyōjin" confirmed Kasuga Ōkami in the right hall. === History === Shrine tradition places its founding in the first month of {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}). This area was Sabi-gō in Ishikawa District, where the Sami clan, descended from the {{ill|lt=Imbe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}}, lived, and Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto is thought to have been enshrined as ancestral deity. It is listed as "Sabi Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}} of {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and ranked as small shrine. It was ranked as village shrine in Meiji 5 and designated a heihaku-offering shrine in Taishō 1. === End shrines === === Sessha === *Suibun Shrine *Tenjin Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) - tradition says it was originally the guardian Daishōgun shrine of Ryūsenzan Castle built by {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}} on Mt. Dake summit === Massha === *Sagami Province Ichinomiya, Samukawa Shrine *Tango Province Ichinomiya, Kagomori Shrine *Tsukisakaki Shrine *Yamashiro Province Ichinomiya, Kamo Shrine *Shinano Province Ichinomiya, Suwa Shrine *Sugawara Shrine *Myōken-gū === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}Sabi 467 ;Transportation * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|lt=4 municipality community bus|Q133808476|WD=Q133808476|1=Kongō Furusato Bus}} "Shimo-Sabi" stop, approx. 2 minutes on foot (about 120 m north) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sabi-jinja}} * [https://sabijinja.site Sabi Shrine Official Site] *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_05_tonndabayasi_sabi.html Sabi Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Association] *[http://www.naniwakagura.sakura.ne.jp/ Sabi Shrine Official Homepage] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:忌部氏|社さひしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2) |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sabi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sabi Shrine''' (さびじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ;Main deity *{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} ;Left side hall *{{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|lt=Matsuo Ōkami|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} **{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} **Nakatsushima-hime-no-Kami ;Right side hall *{{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} **{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} **{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Ihahinushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} **{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} **{{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}} Although the honden is in sansha-zukuri form, documents only listed Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto and Matsuo Ōkami, leaving one deity unknown; however, a repair wooden tag from year 6 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} saying "left Matsuo Daimyōjin, right Kasuga Daimyōjin" confirmed Kasuga Ōkami in the right hall. === History === Shrine tradition places its founding in the first month of {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}). This area was Sabi-gō in Ishikawa District, where the Sami clan, descended from the {{ill|lt=Imbe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}}, lived, and Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto is thought to have been enshrined as ancestral deity. It is listed as "Sabi Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}} of {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and ranked as small shrine. It was ranked as village shrine in Meiji 5 and designated a heihaku-offering shrine in Taishō 1. === End shrines === === Sessha === *Suibun Shrine *Tenjin Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) - tradition says it was originally the guardian Daishōgun shrine of Ryūsenzan Castle built by {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}} on Mt. Dake summit === Massha === *Sagami Province Ichinomiya, Samukawa Shrine *Tango Province Ichinomiya, Kagomori Shrine *Tsukisakaki Shrine *Yamashiro Province Ichinomiya, Kamo Shrine *Shinano Province Ichinomiya, Suwa Shrine *Sugawara Shrine *Myōken-gū === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}Sabi 467 ;Transportation * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|lt=4 municipality community bus|Q133808476|WD=Q133808476|1=Kongō Furusato Bus}} "Shimo-Sabi" stop, approx. 2 minutes on foot (about 120 m north) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sabi-jinja}} * [https://sabijinja.site Sabi Shrine Official Site] *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_05_tonndabayasi_sabi.html Sabi Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Association] *[http://www.naniwakagura.sakura.ne.jp/ Sabi Shrine Official Homepage] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Inbe clan]][[Category:qq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2) |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sabi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sabi Shrine''' (さびじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ;Main deity *{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} ;Left side hall *{{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|lt=Matsuo Ōkami|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} **{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} **Nakatsushima-hime-no-Kami ;Right side hall *{{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} **{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} **{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Ihahinushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} **{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} **{{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}} Although the honden is in sansha-zukuri form, documents only listed Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto and Matsuo Ōkami, leaving one deity unknown; however, a repair wooden tag from year 6 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} saying "left Matsuo Daimyōjin, right Kasuga Daimyōjin" confirmed Kasuga Ōkami in the right hall. === History === Shrine tradition places its founding in the first month of {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}). This area was Sabi-gō in Ishikawa District, where the Sami clan, descended from the {{ill|lt=Imbe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}}, lived, and Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto is thought to have been enshrined as ancestral deity. It is listed as "Sabi Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}} of {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and ranked as small shrine. It was ranked as village shrine in Meiji 5 and designated a heihaku-offering shrine in Taishō 1. === End shrines === === Sessha === *Suibun Shrine *Tenjin Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) - tradition says it was originally the guardian Daishōgun shrine of Ryūsenzan Castle built by {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}} on Mt. Dake summit === Massha === *Sagami Province Ichinomiya, Samukawa Shrine *Tango Province Ichinomiya, Kagomori Shrine *Tsukisakaki Shrine *Yamashiro Province Ichinomiya, Kamo Shrine *Shinano Province Ichinomiya, Suwa Shrine *Sugawara Shrine *Myōken-gū === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}Sabi 467 ;Transportation * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|lt=4 municipality community bus|Q133808476|WD=Q133808476|1=Kongō Furusato Bus}} "Shimo-Sabi" stop, approx. 2 minutes on foot (about 120 m north) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sabi-jinja}} * [https://sabijinja.site Sabi Shrine Official Site] *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_05_tonndabayasi_sabi.html Sabi Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Association] *[http://www.naniwakagura.sakura.ne.jp/ Sabi Shrine Official Homepage] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Inbe clan]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ;Main deity *{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} ;Left side hall *{{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|lt=Matsuo Ōkami|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} **{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} **Nakatsushima-hime-no-Kami ;Right side hall *{{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} **{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} **{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Ihahinushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} **{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} **{{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}} Although the honden is in sansha-zukuri form, documents only listed Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto and Matsuo Ōkami, leaving one deity unknown; however, a repair wooden tag from year 6 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} saying "left Matsuo Daimyōjin, right Kasuga Daimyōjin" confirmed Kasuga Ōkami in the right hall. === History === Shrine tradition places its founding in the first month of {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}). This area was Sabi-gō in Ishikawa District, where the Sami clan, descended from the {{ill|lt=Imbe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}}, lived, and Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto is thought to have been enshrined as ancestral deity. It is listed as "Sabi Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}} of {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and ranked as small shrine. It was ranked as village shrine in Meiji 5 and designated a heihaku-offering shrine in Taishō 1. === End shrines === === Sessha === *Suibun Shrine *Tenjin Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) - tradition says it was originally the guardian Daishōgun shrine of Ryūsenzan Castle built by {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}} on Mt. Dake summit === Massha === *Sagami Province Ichinomiya, Samukawa Shrine *Tango Province Ichinomiya, Kagomori Shrine *Tsukisakaki Shrine *Yamashiro Province Ichinomiya, Kamo Shrine *Shinano Province Ichinomiya, Suwa Shrine *Sugawara Shrine *Myōken-gū === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}Sabi 467 ;Transportation * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|lt=4 municipality community bus|Q133808476|WD=Q133808476|1=Kongō Furusato Bus}} "Shimo-Sabi" stop, approx. 2 minutes on foot (about 120 m north) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sabi-jinja}} * [https://sabijinja.site Sabi Shrine Official Site] *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_05_tonndabayasi_sabi.html Sabi Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Association] *[http://www.naniwakagura.sakura.ne.jp/ Sabi Shrine Official Homepage] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Inbe clan]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2) |本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sabi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sabi Shrine''' (さびじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ;Main deity *{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} ;Left side hall *{{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|lt=Matsuo Ōkami|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} **{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} **Nakatsushima-hime-no-Kami ;Right side hall *{{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} **{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} **{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Ihahinushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} **{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} **{{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}} Although the honden is in sansha-zukuri form, documents only listed Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto and Matsuo Ōkami, leaving one deity unknown; however, a repair wooden tag from year 6 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} saying "left Matsuo Daimyōjin, right Kasuga Daimyōjin" confirmed Kasuga Ōkami in the right hall. === History === Shrine tradition places its founding in the first month of {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}). This area was Sabi-gō in Ishikawa District, where the Sami clan, descended from the {{ill|lt=Imbe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}}, lived, and Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto is thought to have been enshrined as ancestral deity. It is listed as "Sabi Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}} of {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and ranked as small shrine. It was ranked as village shrine in Meiji 5 and designated a heihaku-offering shrine in Taishō 1. === End shrines === === Sessha === *Suibun Shrine *Tenjin Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) - tradition says it was originally the guardian Daishōgun shrine of Ryūsenzan Castle built by {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}} on Mt. Dake summit === Massha === *Sagami Province Ichinomiya, Samukawa Shrine *Tango Province Ichinomiya, Kagomori Shrine *Tsukisakaki Shrine *Yamashiro Province Ichinomiya, Kamo Shrine *Shinano Province Ichinomiya, Suwa Shrine *Sugawara Shrine *Myōken-gū === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}Sabi 467 ;Transportation * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|lt=4 municipality community bus|Q133808476|WD=Q133808476|1=Kongō Furusato Bus}} "Shimo-Sabi" stop, approx. 2 minutes on foot (about 120 m north) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sabi-jinja}} * [https://sabijinja.site Sabi Shrine Official Site] *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_05_tonndabayasi_sabi.html Sabi Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Association] *[http://www.naniwakagura.sakura.ne.jp/ Sabi Shrine Official Homepage] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sabi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Inbe clan]]
